text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil.
zh: 我是Phil。

text: And I'm Bekka.
zh: 我是Bekka。

text: Have you ever done something physically challenging, Phil?
zh: Phil,你有没有做过什么在体力上很有挑战性的事情?

text: Like running a marathon, maybe?
zh: 比如说跑马拉松?

text: Or climbing a mountain?
zh: 或者爬山?

text: I've climbed some mountains, but only very little ones, so it wasn't that challenging.
zh: 我爬过一些山,不过都是很小的山,所以谈不上什么挑战。

text: If physical challenges are something you enjoy, maybe you should try artistic swimming, or synchronised swimming as it used to be called.
zh: 如果你喜欢体力上的挑战,也许可以试试艺术游泳,也就是以前所说的花样游泳。

text: A combination of dance and gymnastics with a difference.
zh: 它融合了舞蹈和体操,但又有所不同。

text: In artistic swimming, athletes dance underwater while holding their breath.
zh: 在艺术游泳中,运动员要一边屏住呼吸,一边在水下跳舞。

text: Did you say dance underwater?
zh: 你是说在水下跳舞?

text: Yes, swimmers dance upside down in the water, holding their breath.
zh: 是的,游泳运动员要倒立在水中跳舞,并且屏住呼吸。

text: As we'll hear, it's an extremely demanding sport, which requires discipline, stamina and years of training.
zh: 我们接下来会听到,这是一项极其艰苦的运动,需要自律、耐力以及多年的训练。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, and you can practice these with the quiz and worksheet on our website.
zh: 像往常一样,我们会学习一些实用的新词和短语,你可以通过我们网站上的测验和练习表来巩固这些内容。

text: That's bbclearningenglish .com Great.
zh: 网址是bbclearningenglish.com,很好。

text: But first, Phil, I have a question for you.
zh: 不过,Phil,我先有个问题要问你。

text: you.
zh: 你。

text: Artistic swimming became an Olympic sport in 1984.
zh: 艺术游泳在1984年成为奥运项目。

text: But which country has won the most gold medal since then?
zh: 但自那以后,哪个国家获得的金牌最多?

text: Is it…
a Russia b China or c the USA Ooh, I think it's b China.
zh: a. 俄罗斯 b. 中国 还是 c. 美国?哦,我觉得是b,中国。

text: Well, we'll reveal the correct answer later in this episode.
zh: 好,我们会在节目稍后揭晓正确答案。

text: Sylvia Solomoshyova from Slovakia is a European Championship medallist.
zh: 来自斯洛伐克的Sylvia Solomoshyova是欧洲锦标赛的奖牌得主。

text: She's also the first artistic swimmer with over a million social media followers after videos of her walking upside down under water went viral.
zh: 在她水下倒立行走的视频走红之后,她也成为了首位社交媒体粉丝超过百万的艺术游泳运动员。

text: Here she explains the different types of swimming dances, called routines, to Dushyane Navagayam, presenter of BBC World Service programme, The Conversation.
zh: 在这里,她向BBC世界服务节目《The Conversation》的主持人Dushyane Navagayam解释了不同类型的游泳舞蹈,也就是所谓的“节目”。

text: If you had to explain artistic swimming to someone who has never seen it, how would you describe it?
zh: 如果要向一个从未见过艺术游泳的人解释这项运动,你会怎么形容它?

text: So I would say it depends if you're watching like a solo or duet or team routine, because in solo there's one person, in duet there's two people, and in team routines there can be eight people or ten people.
zh: 我想说这要看你看的是单人、双人还是团体项目,因为单人项目只有一个人,双人项目有两个人,而团体项目可能有八个人或十个人。

text: And in the team routines there's a lot of lifts and actually in the routines we are smiling but we are hiding behind the smile because we are actually maybe dying.
zh: 在团体项目中有很多托举动作,而实际上在表演过程中我们虽然面带微笑,但其实是在笑容背后苦苦支撑,因为我们可能真的快喘不过气了。

text: We don't have oxygen so but we are smiling and people think that it's easy when it's actually not.
zh: 我们没有氧气可用,但还是要微笑,人们以为这很轻松,但其实完全不是这样。

text: Artistic swimming routines are performed solo meaning by just one person or as a duet which means by two people.
zh: 艺术游泳项目可以是单人完成的独舞,也可以是双人完成的双人项目。

text: There are also team routines where one swimmer is lifted up out of the water.
zh: 还有团体项目,其中一名运动员会被托举出水面。

text: What all these routines have in common is that physically they are incredibly hard.
zh: 所有这些项目的共同点是:在体能上都极其艰难。

text: Yes, artistic routines are done upside down, underwater, without oxygen.
zh: 是的,艺术游泳的动作都是倒立、在水下、没有氧气的情况下完成的。

text: The swimmer's movements need to be as graceful as a ballet dancer.
zh: 运动员的动作必须像芭蕾舞演员一样优雅。

text: And above all, it has to look effortless because judges award points for artistic expression.
zh: 最重要的是,这一切必须看起来毫不费力,因为裁判会根据艺术表现力打分。

text: Sylvia says she hides behind a smile, an idiom meaning to hide pain by presenting a happy outward appearance.
zh: Sylvia说她“躲在微笑背后”,这是一个习语,意思是用看似快乐的外表来掩饰内心的痛苦。

text: To succeed at the top level of any sport requires a huge amount of dedication.
zh: 要在任何一项运动中达到顶尖水平,都需要极大的付出。

text: Sylvia trains eight hours a day, six days a week, under the guidance of her coach, who's also her mum, and a former artistic swimming champion herself.
zh: Sylvia每天训练八小时,每周六天,在她的教练——同时也是她母亲、曾是艺术游泳冠军的人的指导下进行训练。

text: Here, Silvia discusses the sacrifices she's made to succeed with Datiány Navar -Náigam for BBC World Service's The Conversation.
zh: 在这里,Sylvia与BBC世界服务《The Conversation》节目的Datiány Navar-Náigam谈到了她为成功所做出的牺牲。

text: How easy was it being trained and coached by your mum?
zh: 由你妈妈来训练和指导你,这容易吗?

text: I think it's good on one side because you can practise more at home.
zh: 我觉得从一方面来说是好的,因为这样你可以在家里多加练习。

text: But on the other hand, it's not sometimes good because you're training when you're at home, when you're supposed to be, like, chilling.
zh: 但另一方面,有时候也不太好,因为你在家本该放松的时候,却还在训练。

text: There's no respite from it in a way, I guess.
zh: 这样一来,似乎就没有真正的休息时间了。

text: Yeah, I didn't have time for anything else.
zh: 是啊,我几乎没有时间做别的事情。

text: Silvia thinks there are good and bad things about being coached by her mum.
zh: Sylvia认为,由妈妈来指导既有好处,也有坏处。

text: It's good because she can practice at home, but on the other hand, it feels like she's training all the time.
zh: 好处是她可以在家里多练习,但另一方面,也感觉自己好像一直都在训练。

text: She uses the phrase, on the other hand, to show this contrasting opinion.
zh: 她用“另一方面”这个短语来表达这种对比的看法。

text: What Silvia really looks forward to is chilling.
zh: Sylvia最期待的就是“放松一下”。

text: A slang word meaning relaxing or hanging out.
zh: 这是一个俚语,意思是放松或悠闲地待着。

text: For young athletes, balancing sport with school and friends is difficult, and it's hard to get a respite from training.
zh: 对年轻运动员来说,要在运动、学业和朋友之间取得平衡很难,也很难从训练中得到喘息的机会。

text: A respite is a temporary break from doing something difficult.
zh: respite(喘息)指的是从一件困难的事情中获得的暂时休息。

text: Do you know what, Bekka?
zh: 你知道吗,Bekka?

text: I think I need a respite from all this talk about training.
zh: 我觉得我需要从这些关于训练的话题里喘口气了。

text: Now, what was the answer to your question?
zh: 那么,你那个问题的答案是什么呢?

text: Ah yes, Phil.
zh: 啊,对了,Phil。

text: I asked you which country had won the most gold medals for artistic swimming since the beginning of the Olympic sport.
zh: 我问你的是,自艺术游泳成为奥运项目以来,哪个国家获得的金牌最多。

text: And I said I thought it was China.
zh: 我当时猜是中国。

text: Well, you're very close geographically.
zh: 嗯,从地理位置上来说,你猜得很接近。

text: It is, in fact, Russia.
zh: 但正确答案其实是俄罗斯。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with solo – something done alone – and duet – something done by two people.
zh: 好了,我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是solo——独自完成的事情,以及duet——由两个人一起完成的事情。

text: The idiom hide behind a smile means to hide painful feelings by presenting a happy external appearance.
zh: 习语“hide behind a smile”(躲在微笑背后)指的是用看似快乐的外表来掩饰痛苦的感受。

text: The phrase, on the other hand, is used to introduce a contrasting opinion or statement.
zh: 短语“on the other hand”(另一方面)用来引出一个与之前相对比的观点或说法。

text: Chillin' is slang for relaxing.
zh: Chillin'是俚语,意思是放松。

text: And finally, a respite is a break from doing something difficult.
zh: 最后,respite指的是从一件困难的事情中获得的短暂休息。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: But if you'd like to practise the vocabulary you've learnt from this or any of our other episodes,
zh: 不过,如果你想练习本期或其他节目中学到的词汇,

text: then head over to our website, bbclearningenglish .com, for quizzes, pronunciation tips and much more.
zh: 欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,那里有测验、发音技巧等更多内容。

text: Thanks for joining us and goodbye for now.
zh: 感谢大家收听,我们下次再见。

text: Bye!
zh: 再见!

text: 6 Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com
